summary.p: #52 (#Ph27). a) PIM, goddess shrine; b) on the northern side of the main niche of the western façade of the sanctuary; c) personally located and read in situ; d) ARE 1924, no. 393; Tyagarajan (2014: no. 7, 136–137); e) lost regnal year of Tiripuvaṉa Cakkaravarti{{kaḷ Kulottu}}ṅkacōḻatēvar; f) Kulottuṅga I; g) inscription read with G. Vijayavenugopal; h) there is a meykkīrtti of Kulottuṅga I from line 1 to line 7; the first four lines are hardly legible, and for the lines 5 to 7, only the first part of the line is legible; in his edition, Tyagarajan supplies the whole meykkīrtti, but I do not know if he could read this part of the inscription today no longer visible or if he supplied what was expected.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
{meykkīrtti}
marāṉa tiripuvaṉa cakkaravartiTyagarajan reads:kaḷ śrī kolottu after cakkaravarti.
ṅkacoḻatevarkku yāṇṭu
vāṉakovaraiyan cuttamalan UtTyagarajan proposes: (tamacoḻanān ˚ila), the brackets suggesting that he supplies it.
ṅke[śvaran ku][[ṉṟakkūṟṟamāna Uttuṅkatuṅkavaḷa]]
nāṭṭu mannum perum paḻurumur tiruttoṟṟamuṭaiyamahā
devar [k]oyil munpu Iṭṭakaiyāl ceytamatuk ku[lai]ya
m vanta tiruvārātanaiyum inṟi Iruntaṭattu cakkaravaṟ[[ttikaḷ]]
śrī kulottuṅkacoḻadevar tirumeni kalliyāṇa [[ti]]rume
niyāka kulottuṅkacoḻa ˚īśvaramenṟu tirukkaṟṟaḷi [[Eḻunta]]
[[ruḷivi]][ttu tiruvārātanai][[yum]]The line continues to be engraved but Tyagarajan does not mention it and it is covered with cement.
˚aṣṭaparivārālaya topura prākāraṅkaḷ ceyv[[it]]tā[n]
vāṇakovaraiyan cuttamallan Uttamacoḻan [Ila]
ṅkeśvaran ||

{meykkīrtti}. This is the year of Tiripuvaṉa Cakkaravarti{{kaḷ Kulottu}}ṅkacōḻatēvar. Vāṉakōvaraiyan Cuttamalan Ut{{tamacōḻa Ila}}ṅkeśvaran; the temple (kōyil) of Mahādeva of Tiruttōṟṟamuṭaiya of Maṉṉuperumpaḻuvūr (mannum perum paḻurumur > maṉṉuperumpaḻuvūr) of Kuṉṟakkūṟṟam alias Uttuṅkatuṅkavaḷanāṭu having stayed (iruntaṭattu > iruntiṭattu) completely without (inṟi > iṉṟi) worship (vanta tiruvārātanaiyum) when that which was made (ceytamatu > ceytatu) before (munpu) with bricks (iṭṭakaiyāl) was scattered (kulaiyam > kulaiyum); for (āka) the good fortune (kalliyāṇa) of the sacred body (tirumēni), the sacred body (tirumēni) of Cakkaravarttikaḷ Śrī Kulottuṅkacōḻadevar, [he] having caused to raise (eḻuntaruḷivittu) the holy stone temple (tirukkaṟṟaḷi) called (eṉṟu) Kulottuṅkacōḻa Īśvaram, he has made (ceyvittāṉ) the complete worship (tiruvārātanaiyum), the eight subordinate temples (aṣṭaparivārālaya), the gopura (topura > gopura) and the compound walls (prākāraṅkaḷ), [he], Vāṇakōvaraiyan Cuttamallan Uttamacōḻan Ilaṅkeśvaran.
Edited and translated in , based on autopsy and photographs (by Valérie Gillet, 2016-2018).
This digital edition by Valérie Gillet and Emmanuel Francis.
